title: ProviderCard v1
description: Canonical schema for one row in the SOS provider matrix. A ProviderCard names a backend adapter, its tier role (primary/fallback/cheap/premium/local), the model to invoke, optional circuit-breaker thresholds, and a cost estimate. The matrix YAML is validated against this schema at load time so misconfigured providers are rejected before any request is attempted.

schema_version.description: ProviderCard schema version this instance conforms to. Required so v1 and future v1.x / v2 instances are distinguishable at read time.

id.description: Globally unique provider identifier. Must start with a lowercase letter followed by lowercase letters, digits, or hyphens.

backend.description: Which SOS adapter backend executes calls routed to this provider.

tier.description: Role this provider fills in the selection matrix. Used by select_provider() to filter candidates.

model.description: Provider-specific model identifier passed through to the adapter, e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-6'.

endpoint.description: Optional custom API endpoint URL. When absent the adapter uses its default base URL.

health_probe_url.description: Optional URL the health-check subsystem may GET to verify this provider is reachable.

timeout_seconds.description: Per-call timeout in seconds. Minimum 1, maximum 3600.

circuit_breaker.description: Static thresholds for the runtime circuit breaker protecting this provider.

failure_threshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before the circuit opens.

recovery_window_seconds.description: Seconds to wait in the open state before transitioning to half-open.

half_open_max_requests.description: Maximum probe requests allowed in the half-open state.

cost_per_call_estimate_micros.description: Estimated per-call cost in integer micros (1e-6 currency units). Used for budget tracking and tier routing decisions.
